Details: As indicated from batch isotherm tests, adsorption can be very effective (up to greater than 98 percent removal) with bentonite clay or almond materials for cobalt(II) removal. Data for full-scale water treatment or for radioactive cobalt removal were not available; full-scale implementation of this technology may not be feasible. Cobalt removal by adsorptive media is largely dependent on the initial concentration, oxidation state, adsorbent type, and water chemistry (pH and competing anions). Best removals were achieved at pH 6. As pH increased, precipitation played a larger role in cobalt removal.
